architecture.summary: RARE is a retrieval-augmented reasoning framework that decomposes reasoning into sub-questions, retrieves relevant contextual information for each sub-question, re-answers them with the retrieved context, and uses a Retrieval-Augmented Factuality Scorer (replacing a discriminator) to prioritize reasoning paths that meet high standards of factuality.

biblio.title: RARE: Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning Enhancement for Large Language Models

claims.statement: RARE enables open-source LLMs to achieve competitive performance with top closed-source models like GPT-4 and GPT-4o
A Retrieval-Augmented Factuality Scorer is proposed to replace the original discriminator, prioritizing reasoning paths that meet high standards of factuality
RARE leverages information retrieval specifically for generated sub-questions and re-answers these sub-questions with the relevant contextual information
